content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Solving Debugging Bottlenecks for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with elusive bugs. Learn how to use SaaS starter kits, Antigravity, and Claude Code to ship robust, production-ready applications faster.
content.answer_block: Debugging complex SaaS codebases kills your profit margins. By adopting a pre-architected SaaS starter kit, you eliminate structural errors before they happen. Pair this with AI-driven debugging tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to automate root-cause analysis, allowing you to ship features in days instead of weeks.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a screen at 3 AM, trapped in a recursive dependency hell that shouldn't exist. You’re a freelancer, not a glorified bug-fixer, yet your billable hours are being cannibalized by architectural debt and cryptic stack traces. The pressure to ship for your client is mounting, and the complexity of your stack has finally outpaced your manual debugging capacity.

There is a better way. Stop building from scratch and start building on a foundation of battle-tested code. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you shift your focus from fighting syntax to delivering business value. This guide provides the blueprint to reclaim your workflow using the cutting-edge power of Antigravity and Claude Code.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Deploy a robust SaaS starter kit to ensure your authentication, database, and billing layers are pre-configured to production standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new SaaS project using the latest production-grade boilerplate. Implement Auth, Stripe integration, and PostgreSQL/Prisma setup.

steps.title: Automated Code Analysis
steps.description: Use Antigravity to perform a deep-scan of your current codebase to identify potential memory leaks and race conditions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current codebase for architectural anti-patterns and suggest refactors to improve maintainability and performance.

steps.title: Root Cause Isolation
steps.description: When a bug occurs, use Claude Code to trace the execution path and isolate the exact failing module.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Trace the execution flow for the failing endpoint and identify the specific line causing the unhandled promise rejection.

steps.title: Automated Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Prevent regressions by generating comprehensive unit tests for your newly refactored modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Jest unit tests for the identified module, covering edge cases and error states to ensure future stability.

steps.title: Production Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Finalize your workflow by automating the CI/CD pipeline to ensure every commit is linted and tested before hitting production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a CI/CD workflow that runs linting, type checking, and automated tests on every push to main.

pro_tips: Always use 'Strict Mode' in your TypeScript config; it catches 80% of runtime bugs before you even save the file.
Implement 'Feature Flags' in your starter kit so you can toggle experimental code without breaking production for your clients.
Use Claude Code to create 'Context Files' that describe your project architecture; this significantly increases the AI's accuracy in future sessions.
Never manually fix a bug; always ask the AI to explain the root cause first to ensure you are fixing the system, not just the symptom.
Maintain a 'Technical Debt Log' within your repo; if you skip a best practice for speed, log it so Antigravity can suggest the refactor later.
content.next_steps: After mastering this workflow, move on to implementing automated end-to-end testing using Playwright to ensure your client's user experience remains flawless.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting billable hours on avoidable bugs and start scaling your freelance business with a professional, AI-driven stack. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
